title: Conjecture 1.1 (Bandelt and Dress)
statement_text: The maximum quartet distance between two phylogenetic trees on $n$ leaves is $\left(\frac{2}{3} + o(1)\right)\binom{n}{4}$.
context_text: Bandelt and Dress showed that the maximum quartet distance between two phylogenetic trees on $n$ leaves is always strictly smaller than $\frac{14}{15}\binom{n}{4}$ for $n \geq 6$, and conjectured that the ratio between the maximum quartet distance and $\binom{n}{4}$ converges to $2/3$ as $n \to \infty$. The lower bound of $2/3$ is achieved in expectation by the random leaf-labelling argument (Proposition 2.3), so the conjecture asserts that the average and maximum distances are asymptotically equal.
paper_contribution: Improves the best known upper bound from $\frac{9}{10}\binom{n}{4}$ to $(0.69 + o(1))\binom{n}{4}$ using the flag-algebra machinery of Razborov; also establishes the conjecture exactly for the subclass of caterpillar trees, proving the maximum caterpillar quartet distance is at most $\left(\frac{2}{3} + o(1)\right)\binom{n}{4}$.
notes: PDF source — math notation partially garbled in extraction, but the conjecture statement is unambiguous from context.
paper_title: On the maximum quartet distance between phylogenetic trees
